main_turf.bias_notes: A right-handed, flat, oval track. It is the premier racing venue in the South-West of France. Generally very fair with a sweeping final turn and a solid 400m straight.
main_turf.post_bias: Low barrier draws (inside posts) are a distinct advantage in races between 1400m and 2000m, as wide runners lose significant ground on the continuous bends.

handicapping_angles.south_west_hq: As a premier South-West track, local elite trainers (like J.C. Rouget or F. Rohaut) regularly target this venue. Upgrade their runners heavily when returning from tougher Parisian trips.
handicapping_angles.winter_bog: During the autumn and winter months, the track can become extremely deep and holding ('Lourd'). Proven heavy track specialists ('mudlarks') are absolutely essential in these conditions.
handicapping_angles.tactical_positioning: While the 400m straight is fair, the tight final bend often slingshots the leaders. Horses sitting in the 'box seat' (3rd or 4th on the rail) historically excel here, as deep closers can be forced very wide on the turn.
